conclusion: The authors experimentally determined synthesis pathways for higher yttrium hydrides: - $bct\text{-}YH_{4}$ and $bcc\text{-}YH_{6}$ form at 160–175 GPa via laser heating ($\sim 1500$ K) or at 200–244 GPa at room temperature. - $hcp\text{-}YH_{9}$ forms by laser heating $YH_{6}$ with excess hydrogen above 185 GPa. Based on the hydrogen-induced volume expansion ($V_H \approx 1.7-1.8$ Å$^3$/H atom), the experimental compositions are estimated as $YH_{5.7(3)}$ for the bcc phase and $YH_{8.5(5)}$ for the hcp phase.
